g.data-narration: The Object class is the root of Java's class hierarchy. Every class directly or indirectly inherits from Object, making it the foundation of all Java programs.
g.text: The Object class is the root of the Java class hierarchy.
Every class in Java directly or indirectly inherits from Object.

g.data-narration: Object provides common methods that all objects inherit. These methods can be overridden to customize object behavior for toString, equals, and hashCode operations.
g.text: All Java objects inherit these common methods from Object:

g.data-narration: The toString() method returns a string representation of an object. Default implementation returns class name and hash code, but we typically override it for meaningful output.
g.text: Returns a string representation of the object. Override for meaningful output.

g.data-narration: The equals() method compares two objects for equality. Default implementation checks if both references point to the same object in memory.
g.text: Compares two objects for equality. Default checks if references point to same object.

g.data-narration: The hashCode() method returns a hash code value used in hash-based collections like HashMap and HashSet. Equal objects must have equal hash codes.
g.text: Returns hash code value used in hash-based collections. Important contract: equal objects = equal hashCodes.

g.data-narration: The getClass() method returns the runtime class of an object. It returns a Class object containing metadata about the object's actual type at runtime.
g.text: Returns the runtime class of the object. Useful for reflection and type checking.

g.data-narration: The clone() method creates and returns a copy of an object. It's protected, so classes must implement Cloneable and override it to enable cloning.
g.text: Creates a copy of the object. Protected access - must override in subclass. Class must implement Cloneable.

g.data-narration: The finalize() method was called by garbage collector before object destruction. It's deprecated since Java 9 due to unpredictable behavior and security issues.
g.text: Called by GC before object destruction. DEPRECATED since Java 9 - DO NOT USE.

g.data-narration: Overriding equals() and hashCode() is crucial when using collections like HashSet and HashMap. They use these methods to determine object equality and storage locations.
g.text: Essential for Java Collections Framework - HashSet, HashMap, HashTable use these methods.

g.data-narration: getClass() returns exact runtime class for reflection, while instanceof checks inheritance hierarchy. Use getClass() for exact type matching, instanceof for polymorphic checks.
In summary, Object class is the foundation. Key methods to override: toString(), equals(), hashCode(). Always override equals and hashCode together for collections.

g.text: ✓ Object = root of Java class hierarchy
✓ hashCode() - generate unique ID for collections
Exam Tip: Always override equals() and hashCode() together!
